detaileddescription.para: Caller can avoid filling non-needed part of cut faces to speedup cutting still add pseudo FaceId to opposite side to keep connectivity
This enum defines the MR::cutMesh behaviour in case of bad faces acure basicaly MR::cutMesh removes all faces which contours pass through, adds new edges to topology and fills all removed parts

simplesect.para: Bad faces here mean faces where contours have intersections and cannot be cut and filled in an good way

briefdescription.para: determines the weight of each edge in applications like Laplacian
determines the weight or mass of each vertex in applications like Laplacian
Fix method.
The method how to update transformation from point pairs.
The group of transformations, each with its own degrees of freedom.

detaileddescription.para:
Enum class of available CSG operations
MR::boolean

briefdescription.para: Input object index enum.
Face incidence type.
Defines the order of edge collapses inside Decimate algorithm.

detaileddescription.para: If Strong makes additional efforts to avoid creating multiple edges, in some rare cases it is not possible (cases with extremely bad topology), if you faced one try to use MR::duplicateMultiHoleVertices before MR::fillHole
If Simple avoid creating edges that already exist in topology (default)
If None do not avoid multiple edges

briefdescription.para: how to determine the sign of distances from a mesh
resample this grid to fit voxelScale

memberdef.argsstring: (FloatGrid grid, number scaleX, number scaleY, number scaleZ)
memberdef.name: resampled

briefdescription.para: resample this grid to fit voxelScale
returns cropped grid
Performs orthographic projection with of given contours to mesh and cut result lines, fails if any point of contours has missed mesh on projection stage or cut contours contains self-intersections.

simplesect.para: it might be useful to subdivide mesh before cut, to avoid issues related to lone contours

parameterdescription.para: for cutting, it will be changed
for projection onto mesh
to specify direction and contours to mesh space transformation

simplesect.para: newly appeared edges on the mesh after cut or error

memberdef.argsstring: (number radius0, number radius1, number start_angle, number arc_size, number length, number resolution)
memberdef.name: makeCylinderAdvanced

detaileddescription.para:
fills a hole in mesh specified by one of its edge, optionally subdivides new patch on smaller triangles, optionally make smooth connection with existing triangles outside the hole
triangles of the patch

briefdescription.para: creates a mesh of sphere with irregular triangulation
creates a mesh of sphere with regular triangulation (parallels and meridians)
Performs CSG operation on two meshes.

detaileddescription.para:
Makes new mesh - result of boolean operation on mesh A and mesh B
meshA
Input mesh A
meshB
Input mesh B
operation
CSG operation to perform
rigidB2A
Transform from mesh B space to mesh A space
mapper
Optional output structure to map mesh A and mesh B topology to result mesh topology
Input meshes should have no self-intersections in intersecting zone
If meshes are not closed in intersecting zone some boolean operations are not allowed (as far as input meshes interior and exterior cannot be determined)

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh meshA, Mesh meshB, BooleanOperation operation, BooleanResultMapper mapper)
memberdef.name: boolean

briefdescription.para: Performs CSG operation on two meshes.

detaileddescription.para:
Makes new mesh - result of boolean operation on mesh A and mesh B
meshA
Input mesh A
meshB
Input mesh B
operation
CSG operation to perform
rigidB2A
Transform from mesh B space to mesh A space
mapper
Optional output structure to map mesh A and mesh B topology to result mesh topology
Input meshes should have no self-intersections in intersecting zone
If meshes are not closed in intersecting zone some boolean operations are not allowed (as far as input meshes interior and exterior cannot be determined)

briefdescription.para: checks that arbitrary mesh part A is inside of closed mesh part B

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

briefdescription.para: finds all pairs of colliding triangles from two meshes or two mesh regions

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation
if true then the function returns at most one pair of intersecting triangles and returns faster

briefdescription.para: finds all pairs of colliding triangles from two meshes or two mesh regions

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation
if true then the function returns at most one pair of intersecting triangles and returns faster

briefdescription.para: the same as findCollidingTriangles, but returns one bite set per mesh with colliding triangles
finds all pairs (or the fact of any self-collision) of colliding triangles from one mesh or a region

detaileddescription.para: if true then treat touching faces as self-intersections too

briefdescription.para: the same findSelfCollidingTriangles but returns the union of all self-intersecting faces

detaileddescription.para: if true then treat touching faces as self-intersections too

briefdescription.para: Performs mesh simplification in mesh region according to the settings.
Splits too long and eliminates too short edges from the mesh.
Fills hole in mesh

detaileddescription.para: Fills given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face), uses fillHoleTrivially if cannot fill hole without multiple edges, default metric: CircumscribedFillMetric
Next picture show, how newly generated faces can be smoothed MR::positionVertsSmoothly MR::subdivideMesh

parameterdescription.para: mesh with hole
EdgeId which represents hole (should not have valid left FaceId)
parameters of hole filling

briefdescription.para: fill all holes given by their representative edges in

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh mesh, number a, number b, StitchHolesParams params)
memberdef.name: stitchHoles

briefdescription.para: Stitches two holes in Mesh

detaileddescription.para: Build cylindrical patch to fill space between two holes represented by one of their edges each, default metric: ComplexStitchMetric
Next picture show, how newly generated faces can be smoothed MR::positionVertsSmoothly MR::subdivideMesh

parameterdescription.para: mesh with hole
EdgeId which represents 1st hole (should not have valid left FaceId)
EdgeId which represents 2nd hole (should not have valid left FaceId)
parameters of holes stitching

briefdescription.para: Fixes degenerate faces and short edges in mesh (changes topology)
resolves given multiple edges, but splitting all but one edge in each group
finds faces having aspect ratio >= criticalAspectRatio
finds edges having length <= criticalLength
adds in complicatingFaces the faces not from the wedge with largest angle of faces connected by edges incident to given vertex
computes minimal distance between two meshes or two mesh regions

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation
upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid points

detaileddescription.para: This metric minimizes the sum of circumcircle radii for all triangles in the triangulation. It is rather fast to calculate, and it results in typically good triangulations. It is measured in length units.
Same as getCircumscribedFillMetric, but with extra penalty for the triangles having normals looking in the opposite side of plane containing left of (e). It is measured in length units.

Similar to getPlaneFillMetric with extra penalty for the triangles having normals looking in the opposite side of plane containing left of (e), but the metric minimizes the sum of circumcircle radius times aspect ratio for all triangles in the triangulation. It is measured in length units.

This metric minimizes the sum of triangleMetric for all triangles in the triangulation plus the sum edgeMetric for all edges inside and on the boundary of the triangulation. Where triangleMetric is proportional to weighted triangle area and triangle aspect ratio edgeMetric grows with angle between triangles as ( ( 1 - cos( x ) ) / ( 1 + cos( x ) ) ) ^ 4. It is unitless.
Simple metric minimizing the sum of all edge lengths It is measured in length units.

This metric consists of two parts 1) for each triangle: it is the circumcircle diameter, this avoids the appearance of degenerate triangles; 2) for each edge: square root of double total area of triangles to its left and right times the factor depending extensionally on absolute dihedral angle between left and right triangles, this makes visually triangulated surface as smooth as possible. It is measured in length units. For planar holes it is the same as getCircumscribedMetric.
This metric is for triangulation construction with minimal summed area of triangles. It is measured in squared length units. Warning: this metric can produce degenerated triangles

briefdescription.para: Computes combined metric after filling a hole.

detaileddescription.para:
applies given number of relaxation iterations to the whole mesh ( or some region if it is specified )
true if was finished successfully, false if was interrupted by progress callback

applies given number of relaxation iterations to the whole mesh ( or some region if it is specified ) do not really keeps volume but tries hard
true if the operation completed successfully, and false if it was interrupted by the progress callback.

splits edges in mesh region according to the settings;
The total number of edge splits performed

briefdescription.para: returns faces from given region that have at least one neighbor face with shared edge not from the region

detaileddescription.para:
Combines unordered input intersections (and flips orientation of intersected edges from mesh B) into ordered oriented contours with the properties:
Each contour is a. either closed (then its first and last elements are equal), b. or open (then its first and last intersected edges are boundary edges).
Next intersection in a contour is located to the left of the current intersected edge: a. if the current and next intersected triangles are the same, then next intersected edge is either next( curr.edge ) or prev( curr.edge.sym() ).sym(), b. otherwise next intersected triangle is left( curr.edge ) and next intersected edge is one of the edges having the current intersected triangle to the right.
Orientation of intersected edges in each pair of (intersected edge, intersected triangle): a. the intersected edge of mesh A is directed from negative half-space of the intersected triangle from mesh B to its positive half-space, b. the intersected edge of mesh B is directed from positive half-space of the intersected triangle from mesh A to its negative half-space.
Orientation of contours: a. left of contours on mesh A is inside of mesh B (consequence of 3a), b. right of contours on mesh B is inside of mesh A (consequence of 3b).

briefdescription.para: computes size of a cubical voxel to get approximately given number of voxels during rasterization

detaileddescription.para: Offsets mesh by converting it to distance field in voxels using OpenVDB library, signDetectionMode = Unsigned(from OpenVDB) | OpenVDB | HoleWindingRule, and then converts back using OpenVDB library (dual marching cubes), so result mesh is always closed

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh mp, number offsetA, number offsetB, OffsetParameters params)
memberdef.name: doubleOffsetMesh

detaileddescription.para: Offsets mesh by converting it to voxels and back two times only closed meshes allowed (only Offset mode) typically offsetA and offsetB have distinct signs

Offsets mesh by converting it to distance field in voxels (using OpenVDB library if SignDetectionMode::OpenVDB or our implementation otherwise) and back using standard Marching Cubes, as opposed to Dual Marching Cubes in offsetMesh(...)

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh mesh, FaceBitSet region, number offset, BaseShellParameters params)
memberdef.name: mcShellMeshRegion

detaileddescription.para:
Constructs a shell around selected mesh region with the properties that every point on the shall must
be located not further than given distance from selected mesh part,
be located not closer to not-selected mesh part than to selected mesh part.
Offsets mesh by converting it to voxels and back post process result using reference mesh to sharpen features
Offsets mesh by converting it to voxels and back using one of three modes specified in the parameters

briefdescription.para: mapping between original mesh and thicken result

detaileddescription.para: in case of positive offset, returns the mesh consisting of offset mesh merged with inversed original mesh (thickening mode); in case of negative offset, returns the mesh consisting of inversed offset mesh merged with original mesh (hollowing mode); if your input mesh is open then please specify params.signDetectionMode = SignDetectionMode::Unsigned, and you will get open mesh (with several components) on output if your input mesh is closed then please specify another sign detection mode, and you will get closed mesh (with several components) on output;

briefdescription.para: Cuts mesh by given contours.

detaileddescription.para:
This function cuts mesh making new edges paths on place of input contours
mesh
Input mesh that will be cut
contours
Input contours to cut mesh with, find more MR::OneMeshContours
params
Parameters describing some cut options, find more MR::CutMeshParameters
New edges that correspond to given contours, find more MR::CutMeshResult
Input contours should have no intersections, faces where contours intersects (bad faces) will not be allowed for fill
Input mesh will be changed in any case, if bad faces are in mesh, mesh will be spoiled, so if you cannot guarantee contours without intersections better make copy of mesh, before using this function
Mesh to PointCloud

briefdescription.para: Creates mesh from given point cloud according params Returns empty optional if was interrupted by progress bar.
if projector is not given then CPU's computations will be used

detaileddescription.para: Computes signed distances from given test points to the closest point on the reference mesh: positive value - outside reference mesh, negative - inside reference mesh; this method can return wrong sign if the closest point is located on self-intersecting part of the mesh

briefdescription.para: creates simple converters from Vector3f to Vector3i and back in mesh parts area range

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation

briefdescription.para: finds all pairs of colliding edges from one mesh and triangle from another mesh

parameterdescription.para: rigid transformation from B-mesh space to A mesh space, nullptr considered as identity transformation
if true then the function returns as fast as it finds any intersection

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh meshA, Mesh meshB, ContinuousContours contours, boolean getMeshAIntersections, CoordinateConverters converters)
memberdef.name: getOneMeshIntersectionContours

briefdescription.para: if true, then open self-intersection contours will be prolonged to terminal vertices

detaileddescription.para: Converts contours given in topological terms as the intersections of one mesh's edge and another mesh's triangle (ContinuousContours), into contours of meshA and/or meshB given as a sequence of (primitiveId and Cartesian coordinates); converters are required for better precision in case of degenerations; note that contours should not have intersections

briefdescription.para: composes the set of all faces with all their vertices in given set
composes the set of all boundary vertices for given region (or whole mesh if !region)

detaileddescription.para: returns closed loop of region boundary starting from given region boundary edge (region faces on the right, and not-region faces or holes on the left); if more than two boundary edges connect in one vertex, then the function makes the most abrupt turn to left
returns all region boundary loops; every loop has region faces on the right, and not-region faces or holes on the left

memberdef.argsstring: (number primaryRadius, number secondaryRadius, number primaryResolution, number secondaryResolution)
memberdef.name: makeTorus

memberdef.argsstring: (number primaryRadius, number secondaryRadius, number primaryResolution, number secondaryResolution)
memberdef.name: makeTorusWithSelfIntersections

briefdescription.para: converts OpenVDB Grid into mesh using Dual Marching Cubes algorithm

detaileddescription.para: converts mesh (or its part) into a volume filled with signed or unsigned distances to mesh using OpenVDB library; for signed distances the mesh must be closed; prior to conversion, world space is shifted to ensure that the bounding box of offset mesh is in positive quarter-space, and the shift is written in *params.outXf
converts mesh (or its part) into a volume filled with signed or unsigned distances to mesh using OpenVDB library; for signed distances the mesh must be closed; *params.outXf is untouched

briefdescription.para: returns a vector with vertex normals in every element for valid mesh vertices
returns a vector with face-normal in every element for valid mesh faces

detaileddescription.para: creates box mesh with given min-corner (base) and given size in every dimension; with default parameters, creates unit cube mesh with the centroid in (0,0,0)

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on mesh (or its region) to given point

parameterdescription.para: upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exits returning upDistLimitSq and no valid point
mesh-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed
low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one
if provided then only faces from there will be considered as projections
if provided then only projections passed this test can be returned

briefdescription.para: returns the value at given voxel

detaileddescription.para:
sets given region voxels value
region is in grid space (0 voxel id is minimum active voxel in grid)
